relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The line of Esau is named as Edom.
Esau’s household was tied to daughters of Canaan.
His family also connected to Ishmael’s line.
Adah and Basemath bore sons to Esau.
Oholibamah’s sons completed the Canaan-born household.
Esau gathered his household, livestock, and possessions and moved away from Jacob.
Their abundance was too great for them to dwell together.
Esau dwelled in Seir; Esau is Edom.
The genealogy of Esau as father of Edom unfolds in Seir.
The sons of Esau are named.
The sons of Eliphaz branch outward.
Amalek enters the line through Timna and Eliphaz.
Reuel’s sons are named.
Oholibamah’s sons are remembered.
The chiefs of Esau begin with Eliphaz’s line.
More chiefs arise in Edom through Eliphaz.
Reuel’s descendants become chiefs in Edom.
Oholibamah’s sons become chiefs too.
Esau’s sons and chiefs form Edom’s structure.
The older inhabitants of Seir, the Horites, are named.
Horite chiefs are remembered in Edom’s land.
Lotan’s household includes Timna.
Shobal’s sons are listed.
Anah’s wilderness discovery is remembered in the genealogy.
Anah’s children are named.
Dishon’s sons are listed.
Ezer’s sons are listed.
Dishan’s sons are listed.
The Horite chiefs are named.
The chiefs of Seir’s Horites complete their list.
Edom had kings before Israel had kings.
Bela reigned in Edom.
Jobab reigned after Bela.
Husham reigned after Jobab.
Hadad reigned after Husham and was remembered for striking Midian.
Samlah reigned after Hadad.
Shaul reigned after Samlah.
Baal-hanan reigned after Shaul.
Hadar reigned after Baal-hanan, and his household is remembered.
The chiefs of Esau are listed by family and place.
More chiefs are named.
The chief list continues.
The chiefs of Edom are named by dwelling and possession; Esau stands as father of Edom.
